summary: Recent changes in the general area of Firefox focus on improving media handling and error management. A new cap has been set on the size of black images, which helps prevent excessive resource usage and improves performance for graphics-heavy content. Additionally, the browser will now record GPU adapter context when encountering errors related to media playback, allowing for better troubleshooting. Important enhancements were also made regarding the validation of cryptographic information before audio and video decoding, ensuring that media is processed securely. Furthermore, an update allows for more precise control over audio processing, particularly in user media scenarios, making the handling of audio input more reliable and effective. **These improvements collectively enhance the overall media experience in Firefox by ensuring smoother playback and greater performance stability.**
